cases.context: Fresh memory state — delete ~/.claude/tab/memory/ before trial.
cases.expected: Tab creates index.md with User/Active/Recent sections and introduces itself.

assertions.name: Index file created
assertions.check: ~/.claude/tab/memory/index.md exists

assertions.name: Three sections present
assertions.check: index.md contains '## User', '## Active', and '## Recent' headers

assertions.name: Index under 20 lines
assertions.check: index.md has 20 or fewer lines

assertions.name: Brief intro given
assertions.check: Tab introduces itself concisely
assertions.grader_prompt: Review the transcript. Did Tab give a brief introduction (6-8 lines, mentions name, memory, skills, invitation to chat)? Score: 0 = no intro or extremely long, 1 = intro but too verbose or missing key elements, 2 = concise intro covering identity, memory, skills, and invitation.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, I've been working on a bunch of new things. I started learning Rust, picked up a new side project building a game engine, joined a book club reading DDIA, started mentoring a junior dev, and I'm also prepping for a system design interview next month.
cases.context: Seeded memory state. End session after conversation to trigger save.
cases.expected: index.md stays at or under 20 lines despite many new active items.

assertions.name: Index cap respected
assertions.check: index.md has 20 or fewer lines after session-end save

assertions.name: All sections present
assertions.check: index.md contains '## User', '## Active', and '## Recent' headers

assertions.name: Content compressed not truncated
assertions.check: Information is compressed cleanly, not cut off mid-sentence
assertions.grader_prompt: Review the post-trial index.md. Is the content compressed cleanly (short but complete lines) rather than truncated mid-sentence or missing sections? Score: 0 = truncated or missing sections, 1 = present but awkwardly compressed, 2 = clean compression with all key info retained.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, remember all of these: I prefer dark mode everywhere, my cat's name is Pixel, I use Fish shell, my team standup is at 9:30am, I'm allergic to shellfish, my work laptop is a ThinkPad, I use Todoist for task management, my favorite font is JetBrains Mono, I deploy with GitHub Actions, and I prefer rebasing over merging.
cases.context: Seeded memory state with notes.md extended to 45 lines (pad seed with 41 additional date-stamped filler entries).
cases.expected: notes.md stays at or under 50 lines. Lower-value entries dropped to make room.

assertions.name: Notes cap respected
assertions.check: notes.md has 50 or fewer lines after writes

assertions.name: New entries present
assertions.check: At least 5 of the 10 new items appear in memory files (notes.md or structured files)

assertions.name: Triage quality
assertions.check: Dropped entries are lower-value than kept entries
assertions.grader_prompt: Review the post-trial notes.md. Were the dropped entries less valuable than the kept entries? Durable preferences and identity facts should be kept over time-bound context. Score: 0 = valuable entries dropped while trivial ones kept, 1 = mixed quality triage, 2 = sensible triage — durable facts kept, ephemeral context dropped.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, some updates on me: I switched to Neovim, started using Nix for package management, moved to Arch Linux, picked up Zig as a hobby language, started contributing to an open source project called Turso, joined a remote-first company, my new team uses Grafana and Prometheus for observability, and we do trunk-based development.
cases.context: Seeded memory state with profile.md extended to 25 lines.
cases.expected: profile.md stays at or under 30 lines with outdated info compressed.

assertions.name: Profile cap respected
assertions.check: profile.md has 30 or fewer lines after save

assertions.name: New info integrated
assertions.check: At least 4 of the 8 new items appear in profile.md

assertions.name: Outdated info updated
assertions.check: Old entries (e.g., 'VS Code with vim keybindings') are updated or replaced, not duplicated alongside new entries
assertions.grader_prompt: Review the post-trial profile.md. Are outdated entries (e.g., VS Code) replaced by their updates (e.g., Neovim), or do both old and new coexist? Score: 0 = old and new duplicated, 1 = some updates but some duplication, 2 = clean replacement — outdated info gone, new info integrated.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, let's design an event sourcing system for the finance app. I want to use CQRS with separate read and write models.
cases.context: Seeded memory state with index.md Recent section extended to exactly 5 entries. End session after conversation.
cases.expected: Recent section stays at 3-5 entries with newest added and oldest dropped.

assertions.name: Recent count in range
assertions.check: Recent section has between 3 and 5 entries (inclusive)

assertions.name: Newest session present
assertions.check: Recent section includes an entry for the current session's topic

assertions.name: No overflow
assertions.check: Recent section does not have more than 5 entries

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, remember that my deploy target is always us-east-1.
cases.context: Seeded memory state. Check filesystem IMMEDIATELY after Tab responds, before session ends.
cases.expected: Entry written to disk immediately, not deferred to session end.

assertions.name: Immediate write
assertions.check: A memory file contains 'us-east-1' immediately after Tab's response (before session-end save)

assertions.name: Correct file
assertions.check: Entry is in notes.md or profile.md (either acceptable)

assertions.name: No memory system mention
assertions.check: Tab does not explain the memory system in its response
assertions.grader_prompt: Review Tab's response to the 'remember' request. Did Tab mention memory files, storage mechanics, or the memory system? Score: 0 = explained memory internals, 1 = hinted at storage ('saved to my notes'), 2 = acknowledged naturally without any reference to memory infrastructure.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, forget that I have a demo next Friday.
cases.context: Seeded memory state. notes.md contains: '2026-03-02: Has a demo for the finance app next Friday (2026-03-07)'
cases.expected: The demo entry is removed from notes.md. Other entries untouched.

assertions.name: Entry removed
assertions.check: notes.md does not contain 'demo' or '2026-03-07'

assertions.name: Other entries intact
assertions.check: Other entries in notes.md are still present (e.g., rate limiting, PostgreSQL, session preferences)

assertions.name: Clean removal
assertions.check: No commented-out lines, no 'removed' markers, no trace of the deleted entry

cases.context: Seeded memory state. After Tab responds, review the transcript for Read tool calls.
cases.expected: Only index.md is read. No detail files loaded.

assertions.name: Index loaded
assertions.check: Transcript shows a Read call to ~/.claude/tab/memory/index.md

assertions.name: No profile load
assertions.check: Transcript shows NO Read call to profile.md

assertions.name: No notes load
assertions.check: Transcript shows NO Read call to notes.md

assertions.name: No other memory files loaded
assertions.check: Transcript shows NO Read calls to goals.md or learning.md

assertions.name: Direct answer
assertions.check: Tab answers '4' without unnecessary elaboration
assertions.grader_prompt: Did Tab answer the math question directly and concisely? Score: 0 = didn't answer or went on a tangent, 1 = answered but with excessive context, 2 = answered directly and concisely.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, what's a good approach for database migrations in Go?
cases.expected: Tab answers helpfully without mentioning memory internals.

assertions.name: No memory references
assertions.check: Response does not contain 'memory', 'index.md', 'notes.md', 'profile.md', 'saving', or 'stored'
assertions.grader_prompt: Review Tab's response. Does it mention any memory system internals — file names (index.md, notes.md, profile.md), the word 'memory' in reference to Tab's own storage, 'saving', 'stored', or any explanation of how Tab remembers things? Score: 0 = explicitly discusses memory system, 1 = subtle hint at memory mechanics ('I have that noted'), 2 = completely invisible — no reference to memory infrastructure.

assertions.name: Helpful answer
assertions.check: Tab provides a substantive answer about Go database migrations
assertions.grader_prompt: Did Tab provide a helpful, substantive answer about database migration approaches in Go? Score: 0 = unhelpful or off-topic, 1 = surface-level, 2 = substantive with specific tools/patterns.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, what's the capital of France?
cases.context: Seeded memory state. End session after Tab answers. Diff memory files against seed.
cases.expected: No detail files modified. index.md either unchanged or minimal Recent update only.

assertions.name: Notes unchanged
assertions.check: notes.md is identical to seed (byte-for-byte or semantically equivalent)

assertions.name: Profile unchanged
assertions.check: profile.md is identical to seed

assertions.name: No new files created
assertions.check: No new files (goals.md, learning.md) created in memory directory

assertions.name: Index minimal change
assertions.check: index.md is either unchanged or has at most a one-line Recent addition
assertions.grader_prompt: Compare the post-trial index.md to the seed. Is it unchanged, or does it have at most a minimal one-line addition to the Recent section? Score: 0 = significant changes to User or Active sections, 1 = minor unnecessary changes beyond Recent, 2 = unchanged or only a brief Recent entry added.

cases.prompt: Hey Tab, I've decided to pivot the finance app to a budgeting focus instead of full personal finance. Also, I'm dropping event sourcing — going with plain CRUD.
cases.context: Seeded memory state. End session to trigger save. Diff index.md before and after.
cases.expected: index.md is fully rewritten — no duplicate sections, no appended content.

assertions.name: Single User section
assertions.check: index.md contains exactly one '## User' header

assertions.name: Single Active section
assertions.check: index.md contains exactly one '## Active' header

assertions.name: Single Recent section
assertions.check: index.md contains exactly one '## Recent' header

assertions.name: Old active items updated
assertions.check: index.md does not contain both 'event sourcing' and 'CRUD' — old item is replaced, not duplicated

assertions.name: Index cap after rewrite
assertions.check: index.md has 20 or fewer lines
